mission.title: rook: docs: explain "too few PGs per OSD" warning
mission.description: docs: explain "too few PGs per OSD" warning. Requested by 7+ users.

steps.title: Check current rook deployment
steps.description:
Verify your rook version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
helm list -n rook 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working rook installation.

steps.title: Review rook configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant rook configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
kubectl get configmap -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=rook
```
In a lot of scenarios, the ceph status will show something like `too few PGs per OSD (25 < min 30)`, which can be fairly benign.

steps.title: Apply the fix for docs: explain "too few PGs per OSD" warning
steps.description:
Update pg_num related documents.
Commit titles and messages follow guidelines in the [developer guide](https://rook.io/docs/rook/master/development-flow.html#commit-structure).
- [o]
Add the flag for skipping the build if this is only a documentation change. See [here](https://github.com/rook/rook/blob/master/INSTALL.md#skip-ci) for the flag.
- [o]
Add a flag to run tests for a specific storage provider. See [test

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Upgrade rook to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade rook:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade rook rook/rook --namespace rook
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n rook
helm list -n rook
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
kubectl get events -n rook --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "docs: explain "too few PGs per OSD" warning" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Update pg_num related documents.
Commit titles and messages follow guidelines in the [developer guide](https://rook.io/docs/rook/master/development-flow.html#commit-structure).
- [o]
Add the flag for skipping the build if this is only a documentation change. See [here](https://github.com/rook/rook/blob/master/INSTALL.md#skip-ci) for the flag.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with rook installed or the issue environment reproducible.
